items.title: Roku’s 4K 43-inch to 65-inch Select Series smart TVs drop as low as $200 with AirPlay, Apple Home support, more
items.summary:
Amazon is currently offering an affordable means to upgrade to a smarter media experience, starting with the Roku 43-inch Select Smart TV at $199.99 shipped, and you can find 50-inch to 65-inch models on the same page. While it may carry a $250 MSRP, which is already pretty affordable, we haven’t seen the price on this particular size spike higher than $230 in 2026, not to mention that it comes in as an even more super-affordable option for small rooms or for kids over Amazon’s 40-inch 2-series and 43-inch 4-series Fire TV options right now, too. There are some lower-priced options, like the onn 43-inch Class 4K UHD VIZIO Smart TV at $148 shipped over at Walmart, but you’re not getting the upgraded Roku platform inclusion there. The deal here lands it at the third-lowest price we have tracked, beaten out by the drops to $198 and $180.

more…

For multi-camera packs, the only option that lines up with this price is the 4-camera package at $199.99 shipped, which does match the current Amazon pricing. A single camera normally carries a $100 MSRP, but since Black Friday and Christmas sales ended, we’ve only spotted it climbing as high as $65 at Amazon. The deal here gives you another chance to score the solo camera at its all-time lowest tracked price, while the 4-camera package is down at its second-lowest price.

items.title: Score the luck of the Irish with Lectric’s St. Paddy’s Day Sale and up to $694 e-bike savings starting from $799
items.summary:
Lectric is helping you find your commuting pot of gold with its newly launched St. Paddy’s Day Sale that is taking up to $694 off its popular e-bikes and bundled gear. For folks looking to do their rainbow hunting off-road this spring, look no further than Lectric’s XPeak2 Long-Range e-bikes that come with $400 in FREE gear, in either a step-thru design at $1,699 shipped or a high-step design at $1,699 shipped. There’s also the standard step-thru XPeak2 and high-step XPeak2 e-bike options at $1,499 shipped, which come with lesser bundles of $247 in FREE gear. Depending on which you choose, you’d be paying either $2,099 or $1,746 for these e-bike packages without the usual savings on the bundled gear. These bundles break down to include a rear cargo rack, a fender set, and an Elite headlight upgrade for the standard models, while the long-range models get those alongside a suspension seat post and water-resistant pannier bags. Head below to learn more and browse the entire e-bike lineup during this sale event.
